|Details = 
'''The profit rate assumes {{#var:kph}} kills per hour. Your actual profit may be higher or lower depending on your skill, levels, and team.''' An average group of random players should expect roughly 30 minute queen kills (2 kph), including time spent forming a team, and accounting for the occasional player quitting midway through. An average group of players who already have a basic familiarity with their roles should expect 20 minute queen kills (3 kph), this level of proficiency is about what one would have after reaching level 5 in all roles. A more practised group who are good at their roles and employ some advanced techniques should expect to see around 16 minute queen kills (~4 kph), this includes the use of some of the more simple stalls, some coordination between teammates, and the Attacker having high combat stats. Assuming no mistakes and completion of the hard Kandarin Diary, each full clear from waves 1-10 gives about enough points for 1 high gamble.

This guide lists item and stat requirements primarily for the "Attacker" role in Barbarian assault, and assumes you are the team leader. If you are not the team leader, then stall items (mind shields and skillcapes) are not needed. If you are not Attacker, then you will not need any weapons, and can get by with much lower stats. Having at least decent Hitpoints and Defence is still useful regardless of role, as well as wearing more defensive armours. If you are not in a good team, the [[Crystal halberd]] special attack is unlikely to prove useful as it requires ensuring the aggression of the Penance is controlled to allow stacking many of them in one spot.

For more detailed strategies, see [[Barbarian Assault/Strategies]]. It is also recommended to research video guides for more advanced and specific strategies.
}}

The GP per hour depends on how many chests you can get. One chest is worth around {{Coins|{{#var:valueonechest}} round 0}} with [[Morytania Diary|Morytania hard diary]] complete; with max gear and stats, you can complete upwards of 15 chests in an hour, earning around {{Coins|({{#var:valueonechest}}-{{#var:valuearmour}})*15 round -4}} profit without any rare drops.

Using the [[Barrows/Strategies#Equipment|Maximum Efficiency]] setup you can complete around 20 chests an hour. Note, the inputs using [[Barrows/Strategies#Equipment|Maximum Efficiency]] is different from the other methods due to using the [[Tumeken's shadow|Tumeken's shadow]].

Without completing the diary, each chest is worth: {{Coins|{{#var:valueonechest}}-{{#var:valueruneswithdiary}}+{{#var:valuerunes}} round 0}}.

The [[Barrows]] [[mini-game]] is a mid-level [[Combat]] money-making method, completed by slaying all [[Barrows brothers|six brothers]], gathering killcount to maximise reward potential, and looting the [[Barrows chest]] to receive rewards. 

The most effective strategy involves banking at the [[Ferox Enclave]] and using the [[rejuvenation pool]] after each chest, which should lead to runs of approximately 5 minutes. Most runs will [[Barrows chest|reward]] players with roughly {{Coins|1000+{{GEP|Bolt rack|30}}+1.5*{{#var:valuerunes}} round 0}} worth of [[runes]] and [[Bolt racks]], but there is a 1/15 chance of receiving [[Barrows equipment]], which can be sold at the [[Grand Exchange]] for high profit. The strategy guide can be found [[Barrows/Strategies|here]].
}}

Depending on the time of day, you may find it being stocked up to 100-150 in the store at the Varrock Lumber Yard. However, during peak time you may find it more common to see 50 or less in stock, all the way down to 10. This Makes this method have a high variance in profit. You may want to test out different stores, that may be less used for this method. If you spend at most 10 seconds jumping between worlds and purchasing, it allows you to check the store up to 360 times an hour. This leaves you with about 3,600-36,000 nails per hour, assuming a variance of 10 to 100. It may also be outside of this range, but the lower range is fairly pessimistic, as the stores aren't that contested, and you could expect closer to at least 10,000 nails per hour.

|Details = [[Arrow shaft]]s are created by using a knife on [[Logs]]. This can be done from level 1, but better logs provide more arrow shafts as their yield. Beginning with [[Willow logs]], this can be slightly profitable, but with [[Maple logs]], the profit margin increases substantially.

To start with, test the profit of making one batch of arrow shafts; buy one set of maple logs and a knife if needed from the [[Grand Exchange]]. Carve the logs by using the knife on the maple logs, and sell the resulting 60 arrow shafts.

An inventory of 27 logs takes approximately 50 seconds to complete, so players can expect to carve one inventory a minute, accounting for banking time. This results in 1620 logs carved per hour.

If using a [[fletching knife]], more logs can be processed per hour, with each inventory of logs taking about 32 seconds. This can result in upwards of almost 3000 logs per hour.

== Headless Arrows ==
{{main|Money_making_guide/Fletching_headless_arrows}}
If desired, these arrow shafts can be combined with feathers, to be made into [[headless arrow]]s.

Your expected profit will vary depending on your click efficiency.''' After completion of [[Dream Mentor]], players can use the [[Lunar spell]] [[Hunter Kit (spell)|Hunter kit]] and opening the kits to receive various hunter related items. A majority of the profit comes from [[Impling jar]]s, at the current price of {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Impling jar|1}}}} coins. This method allows to open around 1000 [[Hunter kit]]s per hour at high efficiency, while also giving the player approximately {{SCP|Magic|70,000}} experience per hour. }} The current cost of the [[Hunter Kit (spell)|Hunter kit]] spell is {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Astral rune|2}}}} coins. Making it a profit of {{NoCoins|{{#expr:{{GEP|Noose wand|1}}+{{GEP|Butterfly net|1}}+{{GEP|Bird snare|1}}+{{GEP|Rabbit snare|1}}+{{GEP|Teasing stick|1}}+{{GEP|Unlit torch|1}}+{{GEP|Impling jar|1}}+{{GEP|Box trap|1}}-{{GEP|Astral rune|2}}}}}} coins per kit opened. == Strategy == === Casting Hunter Kit === The Hunter Kit spell has a [[tick|3-tick delay]], and the player can only possess one [[hunter kit]] in their inventory at any time.
[[Deposit box]]es can be used at an advantage to deposit the kit between each spell cast by using the kit on the deposit box 3 ticks after casting the spell and waiting 1 tick for the item to be deposited, making this method a 4-tick cycle, meaning up to 1500 hunter kits can be cast per hour.
[[Menu entry swapper|Menu entry swapping]] the "use" option on the hunter kit can be used at an advantage to reduce the amount of clicks required. === Opening kits === Opening the [[hunter kit]]s can be done at a [[bank]]. Opening a kit requires 7 empty inventory slots, meaning up to 3 kits can be opened per inventory.
At high efficiency, it is possible to open 3 kits every 4 to 5 ticks on average by repeatedly clicking on the kits, then depositing the entire inventory at a bank and withdrawing 3 more kits.
The combination of both casting then opening the kits results in up to 1000 hunter kits opened per hour. {{Money making}} [[Category:MMG/Skilling]]

==Non-noting strategy==
''Recommended items: [[Fish barrel]], [[Wilderness sword 4]],  [[Cooking cape]] (optional), [[Rada's Blessing]] (optional), [[Clue box]] (optional), [[Aranea boots]] (optional)''

''Enabling PK Skull Prevention is '''strongly''' recommended.''
{{Inventory
| align = right|Open fish barrel|Lobster pot|Dark fishing bait\54
}}
Although still slower than noting the fish, players can catch and bank dark crabs at a fairly fast rate without Piles if using a [[fish barrel]].

In this setup, players equip a Wilderness sword while bringing an inventory consisting of the fish barrel, a lobster pot and 54 dark fishing bait (28 for the barrel and 26 for the inventory). Note that when the last piece of bait is consumed, the last dark crab will fall onto the ground instead of appearing in the players' inventory; quickly pick it up. After filling an inventory and the fish barrel, run to the [[Deserted Keep]], teleport to [[Edgeville]], bank and repeat. Remember to right-click "empty" on the fish barrel when banking. 

If players chose, they could cook the dark crabs as part of this process. However, this is not recommended as the gp/hour is lower than dark crabs and dark crabs burn easily without a [[cooking cape]]. 

Should the player fall victim to a player killer, they will keep any fish stored in their barrel but will lose their dark fishing bait, Wilderness sword and non-stored crabs. As such, it is recommended to collect several inventories of replacement swords from the [[Lesser Fanatic]] before employing this strategy.

[[Rada's Blessing]] will make runs faster, but due to the inherently unpredictable nature of the double fish bonus it is difficult to predict the exact number of dark fishing bait pieces required when restocking at the bank. As such, players who use it will likely either have extra fishing bait in their inventory or run out before all inventory spaces are filled if less than 54 are used (depending on the success rate of the bonus). Like the Wilderness sword, Rada's Blessing is lost on death. Despite this, the 8% faster fishing will provide the marginal benefit of more fish per hour at the cost of xp per hour since Rada's does not provide xp per catch. 

Players who value clue scroll drops may want to bring a [[Clue box]] to protect any potential clues discovered via [[Clue bottle|clue bottles]] on death. 

'''Note:''' A [[dragon harpoon|dragon]] or [[infernal harpoon]] may be substituted for the Wilderness sword for boosting purposes, but this may slow you down due to the potential to fail the [[Web]] slash. Players can instead boost with the harpoon, re-equip the Wilderness sword and bank the former before leaving Edgeville, though unless you are fast the level boost will have decreased by 1 by the time you start fishing. Alternatively, players may choose to wear [[Aranea boots]] to bypass the [[Web]] slash while wielding a dragon or infernal harpoon; protecting your boots, harpoon, and fish barrel. 
{{Money making}}

The west coast of Land's End features a number of different fishing spots, including a lobster spot, and is closer to a bank than Musa Point. }} {{Money making}}

methods.name: Catching sacred eels
methods.wikitext:
{{Mmgtable |Activity = Catching [[sacred eel]]s |Image = [[File:Sacred eel detail.png|100px]] |Skill = {{SCP|Fishing|87}} (99 recommended)
{{SCP|Cooking|72}} (96 recommended) |Item = * [[Fishing rod]] * [[Fishing bait]] * [[Knife]] * Recommended: * [[Pearl fishing rod]] * [[Rada's blessing]] for increased yield * [[Angler's outfit]] for increased [[Fishing]] experience * [[Spirit flakes]] for 50% increased yield * [[Dragon harpoon]] |Quest = [[Regicide]] |Other = Talk to [[High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa]] |Experience1 = Fishing |Experience1num = 105 |Experience2 = Cooking |Experience2num = 121 |isperkill = y |kph = 220 |kph name = Eels per hour |Input1 = Fishing bait |Input1num = 1 |Output1 = Zulrah's scales |Output1num = 7 |Location = [[Zul-Andra]] |Category = Skilling |SkillCategory = Fishing |Members = Yes |Intensity = Low |Details = '''These calculations assume around {{#var:kph}} catches an hour and 96+ Cooking. At maximum efficiency and concentration, players are able to catch and process up to 260 eels per hour without tick manipulation.''' These calculations are based on having level 99 [[Fishing]], 96+ [[Cooking]], and maintaining a moderate level of efficiency. Assuming the same intensity, a player with level 90 Fishing could expect to catch around 180 eels an hour. Given 96 Cooking, a sacred eel is worth {{Coins|{{GEP|Zulrah's scales|7}}}} on average. Optionally, players may wield their highest available [[Rada's blessing]] for up to 8% chance of catching two eels at once. Similarly, players may opt to use [[Spirit flakes]] as they will increase the yield by 50%. Neither item provides additional [[Fishing]] experience, however, they will increase both profit and [[Cooking]] experience and can be stacked. While the addition of a [[Pearl fishing rod]] does not impact catch rate at maximum efficiency, it frees up an additional inventory slot and may be an option worth considering for players who want to reduce intensity. For players without the [[Pearl fishing rod]], a [[Dragon harpoon]] can be equipped for use of its special attack, which temporarily boosts the player's [[Fishing]] level by 3. Lastly, pieces of the [[Angler's outfit]] may be equipped to gain up to 2.5% additional [[Fishing]] experience. With 99 cooking, a [[chef's delight]] can be consumed to temporarily raise your level to 104, for 8 [[Zulrah's scales]] per cut on average instead of 7. To use this method, deposit the sacred eels in the deposit chest in [[Zul-andra]] after a full inventory and cut them later in a bank. A [[fish barrel]] can make this more AFK but it isn't necessary. Once Cooking drains below 104, it needs to be reboosted; [[Preserve]] is recommended to lengthen the boost. Using this method raises the average value of a sacred eel to {{Coins|{{GEP|Zulrah's scales|8}}}}, which covers the cost of the chef's delights. }} {{Money making}}

Challenge mode Chambers of Xeric is almost the same as normal mode, except the enemies' health and stats are increased. All rooms are also encountered in challenge mode, ensuring that plenty of points and supplies are available. Because all of the rooms are present, scouting is not required, and players barely need to bring in supplies from the outside in challenge mode, as they can easily obtain what they need within the raid.

Nearly all of the profit ({{#expr: 100 * (20000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}} / ((20000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}} + (1 - 20000.0/867580)*(20000*{{#var:valcommon}}+1.0/33*{{GEP|Torn prayer scroll}}) ) round 1}}%) comes from the raid's unique drops. Profit will vary wildly depending on how many and what type of unique drops are received. 

For every 8,676 points obtained, the player has a 1% chance of hitting the unique drop table. A solo deathless raid will generally yield around 62,000 points (57,000 + 5,000 from clearing the raid in time), or roughly a 7.1% chance of hitting the table, equating to a ~1/14.2 drop rate. If the player fails the time, the unique drop rate is reduced to 6.5%, or a ~1/15.3 drop rate.

For every 1000 raids points earned, expect:
*{{Coins|floor((1000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}})}} from unique drops.
*{{Coins|floor((1 - 1000.0/867580)*(1000*{{#var:valcommon}}+1.0/33*{{GEP|Torn prayer scroll}}))}} from common drops
*Totalling {{Coins|floor((1000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}} + (1 - 1000.0/867580)*(1000*{{#var:valcommon}}+1.0/33*{{GEP|Torn prayer scroll}}))}} per 1,000 raids points.

See the [[Chambers of Xeric]] article and the [[Chambers of Xeric/Strategies|Chambers of Xeric Strategy Guide]] for a full description on how to complete raids.

Profit will vary wildly depending on how many and what type of unique drops are received. This guide assumes the player is completing '''normal''' raids and not the [[Chambers_of_Xeric/Challenge_Mode|Challenge mode]] variant

For every 8,676 points obtained, the player has a 1% chance of hitting the unique drop table. A solo deathless raid will generally yield around 30,000 points, or roughly a 3.4% chance of hitting the table, equating to a ~1/33 drop rate, and thus it can be relatively common for players to experience long dry streaks.

For every 1000 raids points earned, expect:
*{{Coins|floor((1000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}})}} from unique drops.
*{{Coins|floor((1 - 1000.0/867580)*1000*{{#var:valcommon}}+1.0/33*{{GEP|Torn prayer scroll}})}} from common drops
*Totalling {{Coins|floor((1000.0/867580)*{{#var:valunique}} + (1 - 1000.0/867580)*(1000*{{#var:valcommon}}+1.0/33*{{GEP|Torn prayer scroll}}))}} per 1,000 raids points.

Each raid consumes roughly {{Coins|floor({{#var:valcost}})}} in supplies.

Try to find a raid that provides a fair amount of supplies; experienced players can forgo most of them completely, but try to fight Olm with an [[Overload (+)]]. If prepping needs to be done, clear rates and overall profit will suffer.

===Strategies===
See the [[Chambers of Xeric]] article and the [[Chambers of Xeric/Strategies|Chambers of Xeric Strategy Guide]] for a full description on how to complete raids.

|Details = [[File:Edgeville dungeon alternative entrance location.png|thumb|right|Go south of Edgeville bank or to the shed west of the Cooks' Guild with a brass key.]] [[File:Edgeville Dungeon map.png|thumb|A map of Edgeville Dungeon.]]
[[Air orb]]s are worth {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Air orb}}}} coins, and they are created by casting [[Charge Air Orb]] on the [[Air Obelisk]] with [[unpowered orb|unpowered orbs]] in your inventory. With an [[air staff]] equipped, this requires three [[cosmic runes]] per cast, costing {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Cosmic rune|3}}}} coins. Using the [[Amulet of glory]] to teleport to [[Edgeville]] for banking, players can charge up to 27 orbs per trip (one inventory slot is needed for the cosmic runes), resulting in a maximum profit of {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Air orb|27}}-{{GEP|Unpowered orb|27}}-{{GEP|Cosmic rune|81}}}} coins per trip.

However, due to [[run energy]] depletion, it can be more worthwhile to withdraw 2 [[energy potion(4)]] and only charge 25 orbs per run. This method yields slightly lower profits per run ({{NoCoins|{{GEP|Air orb|25}}-{{GEP|Unpowered orb|25}}-{{GEP|Cosmic rune|75}}-{{GEP|energy potion(4)|2}}}} coins), but enables higher overall hourly profit due to being able to constantly run. Each trip takes approximately 170 seconds, allowing 21 trips per hour. One trip uses about 75% energy (without [[weight-reducing clothing]]) and roughly 10% energy is recuperated (at level 1 [[Agility]]) while charging the 25 orbs. Consume 6 or 7 doses of energy potion per trip, while running through the dungeon, and bank all leftover doses to be re-combined later. Additionally, [[boots of lightness]] and a [[Spotted cape]] ({{SCP|Hunter|40}}) or [[Spottier cape]] ({{SCP|Hunter|66}}) may be of use, due to their cost and availability relative to other [[weight-reducing clothing]]. 

Alternatively, players with a [[dust battlestaff]] and [[revitalisation pool]] or better in their player-owned house may choose to sacrifice a single inventory slot and bring some Law runes to teleport to their house after each inventory of orbs is charged. This allows the player to use their house to refill run energy and teleport back to Edgeville through an [[Amulet_of_Glory_(mounted)|amulet of glory (mounted)]], thus negating the need to recharge amulets or drink potions. Players who lack access to an amulet of glory may use a [[Ring of dueling]] to teleport to the [[Ferox Enclave]] to recharge and bank, then either use a [[Paddewwa teleport]] — via [[Paddewwa teleport (tablet)|tablet]], [[portal nexus]], or [[portal chamber]] — to get to the [[Edgeville Dungeon]] or run through the [[Ferox Enclave Dungeon]] and use the [[Soul Wars Portal]] twice to get to [[Edgeville]].

It is possible to speed up the trips by charging the orbs manually instead of automatically. To do this, hold the spacebar, click the [[Charge Air Orb]] spell as soon as it becomes available and on the second sound cue of the previous cast, click the [[Air Obelisk]]. Repeat until you have completed your inventory.

The Air Obelisk is located on an island in the [[Wilderness]] only accessible via Edgeville Dungeon. Using the map on the right as a guide through the dungeon, it is recommended to take the northern route through the Wilderness portion of the trek since the [[Chaos druid|chaos druids]] along the southern route can cast a [[bind|binding-like]] spell. There is also an extra gate you must pass through when taking the southern route. It is possible to gain up to 39,900 [[Magic]] experience per hour doing this, making it a notable training method. Players are able to gain moderate experience and profit at the same time.

Bear in mind that running through the Edgeville dungeon places you in [[combat]] range of aggressive [[thug]]s, [[chaos druid]]s, [[deadly red spider]]s, [[skeleton]]s, and [[black demons]], so a good [[Defence]] level or [[food]] could help. '''Since it is in the Wilderness, players should not take anything they are not prepared to lose.''' It is highly recommended to take only 75 cosmic runes per trip to avoid losing a lot of money if you are getting killed.
}}

|Details = After completing the quest [[Dragon Slayer I]], [[Oziach]] will sell the new dragon slayer the fabled [[Rune platebody]] and [[anti-dragon shield]]s at [[Oziach (shop)|his shop]]. He has 35 shields in stock, which can be bought for 26–34 coins, depending on the amount in stock. But the store refills the shields fast enough that it is impossible for a single player to deplete the stock entirely at any moment.

Therefore it is possible to make use of arbitrage, buying low and selling high on the [[Grand Exchange]]. Anti-dragon shields usually sell for a lower price than the guide price on the Grand Exchange. The profit fluctuates a lot because of this. There is demand for this shield, as it is required to make [[dragonfire shield]]s, members need it to kill dragons safely and [[free-to-play]] mages wield this shield, since it the best shield available which has no magic attack penalty.

The method involves a lot of running from the bank in Edgeville to Oziach and returning to the bank to deposit the shields. There are three methods to collect shields. The third method is only available to members.

===Method 1===
If this is done without [[energy potion]]s, you can approximately buy 2,000 shields per hour. This requires concentration and you run to Oziach and walk back to the bank, to make optimal use of your available run energy.

===Method 2===
With energy potions, you can run back and forth from the bank to the shop. This will increase the amount of shields bought to about 3,000 per hour, but that requires 4 doses per trip and about 100 potions per hour. This method could lead to lower profits per hour compared to running without potions in the case the extra profit is lower than the costs of the energy potions.

{{Coins|{{GEP|Anti-dragon shield|3000}}-{{GEP|Energy potion(4)|100}}}} per hour.

===Method 3===
Instead of running back and forth to the store, members can run to the store and then use the [[amulet of glory]] to teleport back to the bank in order to save time and use less running energy. It's unknown how many shields can be bought per hour using this method, as it is untested yet.

===Method 4===
Players who have not completed [[Dragon Slayer I]] can use the [[drop trick]] on [[Duke Horatio]] to obtain large amounts of shields for free. The [[lumbridge bank]] can be used for noting the shields to obtain more.

===Energy conservation and energy restore rate===
Members can wear [[weight-reducing clothing]] to conserve running energy. The skill [[Agility]] increases the restore rate of running energy, but only at high levels the restore rate is high enough to eliminate the need for energy potions. But former members who have trained this skill during their membership, still have increased rates of energy restoration on free-to-play servers.

===Profit per hour===
Using method 1, a player can earn about {{Coins|{{GEP|Anti-dragon shield|2000}}-60000}} per hour using method if the player has an agility level of 1. With method 2, the players earns approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Anti-dragon shield|3000}}-{{GEP|Energy potion(4)|100}}-90000}} per hour, but that depends on the price of energy potions as well.
}}

Players can expect to collect about 550 scales per hour. 70 [[Agility]] is also recommended to avoid having to traverse Taverley Dungeon, which would require a [[dusty key]]. As indicated on the map, follow the sky-blue route to the dungeon entrance. Enter the obstacle pipe if you have 70 Agility, or otherwise, go the long way around (see map above for details), but this is tedious and will only result in around 375 scales per hour. methods.name: Collecting chaos runes
methods.wikitext:
{{MMGWarning|Takes place in the [[Wilderness]]}} {{Mmgtable |Activity = Collecting [[chaos rune]]s within the [[Dark Warrior's Fortress]] |Image = [[File:Dark Warriors' Fortress.png|300px]] |Skill = Decent {{SCP|Combat}} recommended
{{SCP|Magic|25}} to teleport to Varrock |Item = * [[Armour]] (optional) * [[Food]] to survive attacks from hostile NPCs and [[PKer]]s * [[Law rune|Law]] and [[air rune]]s to [[Varrock Teleport|teleport to Varrock]] * [[Energy potion]]s |Other = None |Quest = None |isperkill = y |kph = 1500 |kph name = Chaos runes per hour |Output1 = Chaos rune |Other Benefits = None |Category = Collecting |Members = No |Intensity = Moderate |Location = [[Dark Warriors' Fortress]] |Details = [[Chaos rune]] spawns, along with a number of lesser runes, can be found in level 15 [[wilderness]], located north of the [[Ice Mountain]]. The fortress is populated with level 8 [[dark warrior]]s that will hit you almost anywhere on the bottom floor because it is multi-way combat. Remember, the [[dark warrior]]s are only level 8 in free-to-play worlds, and level 145 in member worlds, so use only free-to-play worlds unless you feel suited to fight very high level enemies. Thus, it is advised to wait in the safe 3rd floor, via one of the ladders in the corner, for 10 minutes prior to starting this money making method so the warriors are no longer aggressive to you. If you don't want to wait, or have high enough combat to take no damage, then head to the north-west room to begin. With full attention, you should be able to grab from 1,400 to 1,600 runes per hour. Keep in mind that running into a [[player killer]] means potentially losing all your profits, it is advisable to take a [[teleport]] spell with you for a quick getaway. While you could pick up the other runes, it's optimal to grab the 5 chaos runes and immediately switch worlds, then repeat, since they're worth far more than the rest. The chaos rune has a respawn time of 2 minutes. It could be worth taking 3-5 pieces of food, but is not needed. To maximise profits, it is recommended to change worlds after picking up the runes. Without changing worlds, one can expect to only pickup 150 runes per hour. }} {{Money making}}

methods.name: Collecting wine of zamorak
methods.wikitext:
{{Mmgtable |Activity = Collecting [[wine of Zamorak]] |Image = [[File:Chaos_Temple (Asgarnia) interior.png|300px]] |Skill = {{SCP|Magic|33}}
{{SCP|Hitpoints|20+}} recommended
500 [[total level]] |Item = * [[Staff of air]] * [[Zamorak robes]] * [[Law rune]]s * [[Cake]] * [[Restore potion]] * [[Looting bag]] (Recommended) * [[Games necklace]]s (Members) * [[Ring of dueling|Rings of dueling]] (Members) |Quest = None |Other = None |Other Benefits = {{SCP|Magic|11,160}} |Experience1 = Magic |Experience1num = 43 + 48/53 |isperkill = y |kph = 212 |kph name = Wines per hour |Input1 = Law rune |Input1num = 1 + 1/53 |Input2 = Cake |Input2num = 32/212 |Input3 = Restore potion(4) |Input3num = 4/212 |Input4 = Water rune |Input4num = 1/53 |Input5 = Ring of dueling(8) |Input5num = 1/53/8 |Output1 = Wine of zamorak |Output1num = 1 |Location = [[Chaos Temple (Asgarnia)|Chaos Temple]] (Free-to-play, above Goblin Village) |Members = Yes |Category = Collecting |Intensity = Moderate |Details = [[File:Collecting wine of zamorak map.png|right|100px]] [[Wine of zamorak|Wines of Zamorak]] are used to create [[ranging potion|ranging]] and [[bastion potion]]s. They spawn on two tables in the [[Chaos Temple (Asgarnia)|Chaos Temple]], which is located north-west of [[Goblin Village]], guarded by [[Monk of Zamorak|Monks of Zamorak]]. Wine of Zamorak can be taken from the table downstairs, which will cause the monks to attack you, even when you use the [[Telekinetic Grab]] spell. However, a table with a second Wine of Zamorak spawn can be accessed by climbing up the ladder by the entrance, which can be taken safely by use of the Telekinetic Grab spell. Climbing the ladder requires you to have a [[total level]] of 500 while wearing a set of [[Zamorak robes]]. Wield a [[staff of air]] (any staff that grants unlimited air runes works) and Zamorak robes. Your inventory should consist of 53 [[law rune]]s, 8 [[cake]]s, a single [[restore potion]], and a [[looting bag]]. (Members may eschew the cakes and restore potions by using [[Ring of dueling|rings of dueling]], and using the [[Pool of Refreshment]] at the [[Ferox Enclave]]. This can also work as your banking teleport). Enter the Chaos Temple by walking north from [[Falador]]; members can use [[games necklace]]s to go to Burthorpe and utilise a shortcut unlocked by the [[easy Falador Diary]] to get there faster. Climb the ladder and use [[Telekinetic Grab]] on the wine on the table, then climb back down the ladder. Use Telekinetic Grab once more on the wine across the room to quickly grab the other [[Wine of Zamorak]]. Once you get the Wine of Zamorak, quickly go up the ladder. Wait for the first wine to respawn, then repeat the process. Be careful, as the monks' attacks can drain your combat stats and health quickly. Eat as needed and use a dose of your potion if your magic stat gets too low to cast Telekinetic Grab. Both wines will respawn 25 seconds after they are taken. Once you have a full inventory, enter the [[Wilderness]] just to the north and fill your looting bag to its maximum capacity. Resume grabbing wines until your inventory is full again, then use your [[Closest...|closest]] bank teleport, or [[Closest.../Free-to-play#Banking|closest free-to-play]] bank, to store your wine. It is recommended to drop excess restore potions and cakes you didn't use to make more room for wine. You can do a run of 53 wines in 15 minutes with the looting bag, or 204 per hour, for a profit of {{Coins|{{GEP|Wine of zamorak|204}}-{{GEP|Law rune|204}}}}. If you walk between [[Falador west bank]] and the [[Chaos Temple (Asgarnia)]], you can collect 192 wines per hour with the looting bag, for a profit of {{Coins|{{GEP|Wine of zamorak|192}}-{{GEP|Law rune|192}}}}. On [[free-to-play]] worlds, both spawns may be crowded. You can either switch worlds until you find an empty temple, or compete with the other players to get the wine first, although competing will likely cause both players' profit per hour to drop. If competing, note that whether players standing on the same spot are clickable depends on your own orientation. Telegrabbing the wine will make you face the table, and your staff will hover over the table, making it much easier to misclick on a player. }} {{Money making}} [[Category:MMG/Collecting]]

|Details = '''The profit rate assumes 205 shades cremated (and their keys opened) per hour. Your actual profit may be higher or lower depending on your speed and luck. The profit also assumes [[Mimic | Mimics]] are activated and defeated on the first attempt.'''

Cremating urium remains, collecting the keys and opening the Gold Chests in the [[Shade Catacombs]] gives on average one [[clue scroll (elite)|elite clue]] every 35 minutes, in addition to other standard loot (mostly coins and alchables). It also gives a decent amount of Firemaking and Prayer experience, and chances at the [[Zealot's robes]], which are useful for training Prayer.

For free transportation, [[Morytania legs 3]] or better is the best way to get to Mort'ton regularly. [[Mort'ton teleport]]s are also an option, but at {{Coins|{{GEP|Mort'ton teleport}}}} each they are fairly expensive and will reduce the hourly profit by a lot, for only 20 or so seconds saved per trip. The [[Grouping|minigame teleport]] to [[Shades of Mort'ton (minigame)|Shades of Mort'ton]] is also a good option, but has a cooldown timer of 20 minutes.

To cremate the shades, fill up the [[gold coffin]] with 28 urium remains and fill the rest of the inventory with [[redwood pyre logs]] and a [[tinderbox]]. Use the logs and remains on the pyre and light it, collecting the reward (either coins or a gold key) from the pedestal. You can stack up multiple rewards on the pedestal to save time, but coins and keys will disappear after 5 minutes. [[Tick manipulation]] methods can be used to slightly increase the speed the shades are cremated, up to around 400 cremations + loots per hour, or 600 cremations without chests. This is heavily decreased by completing the elite clues you gather.

You can either bank the keys or go open chests directly from there. They are about the same overall speed, although saving up keys can lead to more efficient chest opening because you won't have to switch chest types as often and instead of running a partial inventory of keys while getting coin drops, more keys can be used in one run.

Either way, enter the [[Shade Catacombs]], go through the gold door, and start opening chests. Each loot will give coins, swamp paste, and one other item – none of them are incredibly valuable (besides the elite clue), but they all add up to make each chest well over {{Coins|10,000}} on average.
}}

Your expected profit will vary depending on your kills per hour.''' Also take into consideration how quickly you can get through the dialogue, get the splinters, and reset. Have the items you need to re-supply with easily accessible. While it may only take 20-30 seconds to clear the wave, it does take another 20-30 seconds to reset. The Bulwark method relies on the [[Dinh's bulwark]] special attack to inflict heavy damage on all the enemies present and prevent the [[Jaguar warrior]] from spawning. It requires tanking the range and melee Fremenniks so high melee stats (80+) are necessary for consistent completions. Having maxed melee stats will increase the number of completions per hour. The range/mage method heavily relies on the [[Area of effect|AOE]] damage potential of the [[Venator bow]] and [[Barrage spells]] for fast wave completions and to prevent the [[Jaguar warrior]] from spawning, it is highly recommended to use both. One dose of [[Forgotten brew]] may be used to boost your magic level at the start of each wave if you don't meet the requirements for Barrage spells. You should not take any food or prayer restoration potions as leaving after claiming rewards restores your health, prayer, and run energy, though it will also reset any boosted stats to their original level. Special attack energy will not be restored when leaving, but it will be restored when entering the Colosseum. ===Bulwark Method=== Requires tanking of range and melee Fremenniks * Enter the Colosseum with melee gear and a Dinh's bulwark * Drink one dose of super attack and strength potions * Pick the Blasphemy handicap and start the wave * Pray [[Protect from magic]] and [[Piety]] before the wave starts, and turn on your special attack * Wait till the Fremenniks are less than 5 tiles away, then attack the [[Serpent shaman]] to hit all 4 enemies at once * After two special attacks, switch to your main melee weapon and finish off the remaining enemies * Claim your reward and repeat ===Range/Mage Method (Safe)=== You should not take any major damage using this method * Enter the Colosseum with magic gear * Drink one dose of ranging potion * Pick the Blasphemy handicap and start the wave * Pray [[Protect from magic]] and [[Augury]] before the wave starts * Cast [[Ice barrage]] twice on the [[Fremennik warband berserker]] (middle one) as soon as the wave starts * Switch to ranged gear, equip the [[Venator bow]] and pray [[Rigour]] * Attack the [[Fremennik warband archer]] until one of the remaining Fremenniks or both die * Finish off the last Fremennik and/or the [[Serpent shaman]] with the [[Toxic blowpipe]] and/or dump [[Special attacks]] * Claim your reward and repeat ===Range/Mage Method (Advanced)=== You may need to tank one or two melee hits from the berserker * Enter the Colosseum with magic gear * Drink one dose of ranging potion * Pick the Blasphemy handicap and start the wave * Pray [[Protect from magic]] and [[Augury]] before the wave starts * Cast [[Blood barrage]] (or your strongest barrage spell that is not ice) on the [[Fremennik warband berserker]] (middle one) as soon as the wave starts * Run next to the [[Serpent shaman]] and prepare to cast another barrage on the berserker as soon as you are one tile next to the shaman * Switch to ranged gear, equip the [[Venator bow]] and pray [[Rigour]] * Attack the Serpent shaman until only one enemy remains * Finish off the last enemy with the [[Toxic blowpipe]] and/or dump [[Special attacks]] * Claim your reward and repeat Both of these methods should average consistent wave 1 completions in 20-30 seconds, or 50 seconds if the jaguar warrior ends up spawning. Your completion speed may vary based on your performance, stats and equipment used. }} {{Money making}}

Depending on the method of travel, a round trip to the [[Cosmic Altar]] takes about 45 seconds to a minute, including banking and filling up [[essence pouch]]es.

To begin the player will need to build a fairy ring and [[Revitalisation pool]] or greater in their [[POH]]; Additionally, a [[rune pouch]] filled with astral and cosmic runes will save inventory space. Once the runes have been crafted, use an equipped [[ring of dueling]] to bank at [[Castle Wars]] before using a [[teleport to house (tablet)]] or [[Construct. cape]] and use your fairy ring to travel Zanaris, refilling run energy at your revitalisation pool as needed.

Alternatively, a [[quest point cape]] can be used to access fairy rings after banking.

'''Note:''' With the [[Runecraft cape]] it is possible to reach up to 83 trips per hour, increasing your profit.

}}

Depending on the method of travel, a round trip to the [[Cosmic Altar]] takes about a minute and a half to two minutes, including banking and filling up [[essence pouch]]es. To start players must collect and pay for some [[scarred extract]] from the [[scar essence mine]], collect them at a ratio of 1 per 61 [[aether catalyst]].

Using the [[Slayer ring]] and [[fairy rings]], you can make about 40 trips to the Cosmic Altar per hour. Start out at the [[Edgeville]] bank. Unload pure essence and store it in your pouches, and then take out essence for your inventory. Teleport via the slayer ring to [[Rellekka Slayer Caves]], exit, and use the fairy ring to the west to teleport to [[Zanaris]]. From there, run south to the [[Agility shortcut]]; if you have 66 [[Agility]], go through both of them, although you may get stuck and take a small amount of damage before passing through. Run further south and enter the mysterious ruins, and then go directly to the altar. Craft your essence in your inventory and pouches, and then teleport back to Edgeville with your [[amulet of glory]] or though player owned house.

Players with 80 [[Construction]] can use a fairy ring in their house for transport to Zanaris, in excess of 45 trips per hour are achievable when using this option. To begin the player will need to build a fairy ring, [[Lunar altar]], and [[Revitalisation pool]] or greater in their [[POH]]; a [[basic jewellery box]] or greater is also useful but optional. Additionally, a [[rune pouch]] filled with air, astral, and cosmic runes will save inventory space. Once the runes have been crafted, use an equipped [[ring of dueling]] to bank at [[Castle Wars]] before casting [[Teleport to House]] and use your fairy ring to travel Zanaris, refilling run energy at your revitalisation pool as needed. The lunar altar will allow swapping to the [[Lunar spellbook]] briefly to cast NPC Contact when essence pouches need repairing, remember to switch back to the normal spellbook with the altar again afterwards.

Alternatively, a [[quest point cape]] or [[necklace of passage]] ([[Wizards' Tower]] teleport) can be used to access fairy rings after banking in Edgeville.

'''Note:''' Due to the large quantity of aether catalysts required for this method in conjunction with their [[Grand Exchange/Buying limits|buy limit]] of 25,000, you may need to wait for well over a day to be able to buy enough catalysts from the [[Grand Exchange]] in order to sustain just a couple of hours of making money with this method. This can be mitigated by buying the catalysts across multiple accounts or buying them directly from other players.

}}

Using the [[Moonclan Teleport]] (requiring 69 [[Magic]]), a round trip from the [[Lunar Isle]] bank to the [[Astral Altar]] takes about 49 seconds, including banking and filling up [[essence pouch]]es, allowing 73 trips per hour to be made. Alternatively, the [[Tele Group Moonclan]] spell (requiring 70 [[Magic]]) will teleport the player a few tiles closer to the bank. This can be further reduced to about 34 seconds using [[teleport to boat]] to a boat docked at [[Lunar Isle]] and using another teleport for banking (e.g. [[crafting cape]], [[sailor's amulet]] to [[Deepfin Point]]). Teleport to boat also has the advantage of making the altar visible as soon as you teleport, making it a single click to craft the first set of runes.
To start out, players should withdraw a full inventory's worth of [[pure essence]] from the bank, and then fill their pouch(es) with the remaining essence; this should come out to 66 essence per trip if utilising the colossal essence pouch and rune pouch. Next, run outside heading south and then east past the [[Suqah]]s, trying to avoid being hit. With 43 [[Prayer]], you can [[flick]] [[protect from melee]] to avoid taking any damage or losing any prayer points as you run past them. Otherwise, you may have to consume food or prayer potions occasionally, reducing profit per hour slightly. Once at the Astral Altar, craft your runes and use the moonclan teleport to bank, repeating the whole process. Using a [[crafting cape]] to bank at the [[crafting guild]] may potentially be slightly faster, however it is unlikely to increase profit if it takes up an inventory slot that could be used to carry essence instead, and wearing a weight-reducing cape is likely to be better.
If not wearing a Runecraft cape, you should use the [[NPC Contact]] spell to contact the [[Dark Mage (Abyss)|Dark mage]] to repair your essence pouch(es) whenever they degrade. If using a rune pouch, you should store law, cosmic, and astral runes inside it, which along with a dust battlestaff, will cover your teleports to bank as well as your contact spells to repair pouches. If not using a rune pouch, you should either carry law runes or moonclan teleport tablets to cover your teleports to bank, keeping cosmic runes in the bank and withdrawing one each time you need to repair your essence pouch(es). Using teleport tablets is slightly less click-intensive, however it will likely lower your profit per hour. The current difference in profit per hour is {{Coins|{{GEP|Astral rune|2*73}}+{{GEP|Law rune|73}}-{{GEP|Moonclan teleport (tablet)|73}}}}
While wearing the recommended clothing, each trip will deplete your stamina by approximately 40%, so drink a 2-dose [[stamina potion]] while filling essence pouches at the bank (completion of [[Alfred Grimhand's Barcrawl]] will allow you to automatically destroy the empty vial upon finishing the potion). Slower alternatives are teleporting to [[Nardah]] with [[desert amulet 4]] and praying at the [[Elidinis Statuette]], using a [[Teleport to House]] tab or [[Spellbook Swap]] to drink from your [[Revitalisation pool]], or using the [[Pool of Refreshment]] located in the [[Ferox Enclave]] via a [[ring of dueling]]. Depending on the cost of stamina potions and how quickly you are able to perform these alternatives, it may be possible for them to be more profitable. }} {{Money making}}

|SkillCategory = Runecraft
|Details = At average speeds, while wearing the Raiments of the Eye outfit, one can do about 7.5 trips to the [[Blood Altar (Kourend)|Blood Altar]] per hour, producing a base 188 blood runes each trip.

Players should mine an inventory full of [[dense essence block]]s at the [[dense runestone mine]], run to the [[Dark Altar]] to turn them into [[dark essence block]]s, and chip them with a chisel into [[dark essence fragments]]. Then, players should run back to the dense runestone mines and mine another inventory full of dense essence blocks, turn them into dark essence blocks, and run to the Blood Altar south-west of the dark altar.

Once there, craft the fragments in the inventory into runes, chisel the remaining blocks, and runecraft them as well. Run back to the dense runestone mine (with 73 Agility, there is a [[shortcut]] available) and repeat.

Wearing a full set of [[Raiments of the Eye]] grants the player 60% additional runes when crafting blood runes, adding to the profit made from this method. No additional experience is gained, however. It is advisable to bring a [[Ring of Endurance]] with at least 500 charges, as it will reduce the drain rate for run energy by 15%.

Completing the [[Kourend & Kebos Diary#Medium|medium Kourend & Kebos Diary]] grants the player a 5% chance of mining an additional dense essence block, adding to the profit made from this method. No additional mining/crafting experience is awarded for the extra block.

Completing the [[Kourend & Kebos Diary#Elite|elite Kourend & Kebos Diary]] grants the player 10% additional runes when crafting blood runes, adding to the profit made from this method. No additional experience is gained, however.

Bringing along an activated [[blood essence]] grants a 50% chance to craft an extra blood rune per dark essence fragment for no additional experience, for a profit of {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Blood rune|1000}} - {{GEP|Blood essence}}|c}} per blood essence if used. Due to losing an inventory slot, this equates to 44% bonus runes per/hour and -4% Experience p/h. If bringing a stack of unactivated essence also, expect 38% bonus runes and -8% Experience p/h.
}}

|Details = {{Map:AbyssRoutes}} {{Map:Abyss_Inner_Ring}}
At average speed, one can do about 50–60 trips to the [[Blood Altar]] via the [[Abyss]] per hour, using the [[amulet of glory]] to teleport back to [[Edgeville]], and using an equipped [[pickaxe]] to mine through the rocks in the Abyss. You need to recharge your pouches every eight trips, at the [[Dark Mage (Abyss)|Dark mage]], to prevent the colossal pouch from degrading.

The [[Mage of Zamorak]] is located in low-level [[Wilderness]], near the source of the [[River Lum]]. This is a fairly safe area, but there might be [[player killer]]s, so it is advised not to take expensive items on the trips to the Abyss. The best setup would be cheap but protective gear (e.g. [[dragonhides]]). Use of [[weight-reducing clothing]] is also recommended. [[Falador shield]] can be useful to negate the loss when killed by a player, as it can recharge prayer instantly (once or twice per day) which can be used to activate [[Protect Item]]. Use of [[abyssal bracelet]] is optional to prevent [[skulling]] when entering the abyss. Inside the Abyss, the creatures there will be aggressive and might do fair amounts of damage. See [[Abyss#Inside the Abyss]] for more information on how to navigate the Abyss. Once inside, enter the blood rift and craft the essence in your inventory and your pouches. Teleport back to Edgeville with an amulet of glory and repeat.

Wearing a full set of [[Raiments of the Eye]] grants the player 60% additional runes when crafting blood runes, adding to the profit made from this method. No additional experience is gained, however.

[[Blood essence]] provides a 50% chance to craft additional runes per essence used for no additional experience, resulting in a profit of {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Blood rune|1000}} - {{GEP|Blood essence}}|c}} per blood essence if used. Only recommended if profitable.

A [[ring of dueling]] is optional, but it helps in case players forget to swap out an exhausted glory for a charged one. Using [[Charge dragonstone jewellery scroll]] to recharge your amulets is recommended as it is quick, allowing you to spend more time runecrafting.

The [[achievement diary cape]] is especially useful for runecrafting in the Abyss if the player has one. Teleport to [[Jarr]] at [[Shantay Pass]] for banking, then to the [[Lesser Fanatic]] to quickly reach the Abyss. Since an amulet of glory isn't needed for this method, the player could instead opt to wear a [[Desert amulet 4]] and teleport to [[Nardah]] when necessary to replenish run energy and [[hitpoints]] without the need for stamina potions and food.
}}

Depending on the method of travel, a round trip to the [[Cosmic Altar]] takes about a minute and a half to two minutes, including banking and filling up [[essence pouch]]es. To start out, players should withdraw a full inventory's worth of [[pure essence]] from the bank, and then fill their pouches with the remaining essence; this should come out to 66 essence per trip if utilising the [[colossal pouch]], which will have to be repaired by use of the [[NPC Contact]] spell (67 [[Magic]]) to contact the [[Dark Mage (Abyss)|Dark mage]] as they degrade. At the bare minimum, profits can get as low as {{Coins|{{Formatexpr|27*30*({{GEP|Cosmic rune}}-{{GEP|Pure essence}})}}}} assuming 810 essence is bought and then crafted per hour.

Using the [[Slayer ring]] and [[fairy rings]], you can make about 40 trips to the Cosmic Altar per hour. With 59 [[Runecraft]] (giving double [[cosmic rune]]s) and especially 85 Runecraft (giving access to the highest capacity of the [[colossal pouch]]), this can be highly profitable. Start out at the [[Edgeville]] bank. Unload pure essence and store it in your pouches, and then take out essence for your inventory. Teleport via the slayer ring to [[Rellekka Slayer Caves]], exit, and use the fairy ring to the west to teleport to [[Zanaris]]. From there, run south to the [[Agility shortcut]]; if you have 66 [[Agility]], go through both of them, although you may get stuck and take a small amount of damage before passing through. Run further south and enter the mysterious ruins, and then go directly to the altar. Craft your essence in your inventory and pouches, and then teleport back to Edgeville with your [[amulet of glory]]. With this method, you can craft about 2,640 essence per hour. Without the colossal pouch, that will drop to about 2,160.

Alternatively, a [[quest point cape]] or [[necklace of passage]] ([[Wizards' Tower]] teleport) can be used to access fairy rings after banking in Edgeville.

Players with 80 [[Construction]] can use a fairy ring in their house for transport to Zanaris, allowing more than 45 trips per hour. To begin the player will need to build a fairy ring, [[Lunar altar]], and [[Revitalisation pool]] or greater in their [[POH]]; a [[basic jewellery box]] or greater is also useful but optional. Additionally, a [[rune pouch]] filled with law, astral, and dust runes will save inventory space. Once the runes have been crafted, use an equipped [[ring of dueling]] to bank at [[Castle Wars]] before casting [[Teleport to House]] and use your fairy ring to travel Zanaris, refilling run energy at your revitalisation pool as needed. The lunar altar will allow swapping to the [[Lunar spellbook]] briefly to cast NPC Contact when essence pouches need repairing, remember to switch back to the normal spellbook with the altar again afterwards.
}}

essence |26 = Pure essence |27 = Pure essence |28 = Rune pouch }} {{Rune pouch | align = right | 1 = Astral rune | 2 = Cosmic rune | 3 = Nature rune }} '''Note for Ironmen: [[Ali's Discount Wares]] will buy unlimited [[nature runes]] for {{Coins|90}} each. Assuming 125 trips per hour, this method produces {{Coins|{{#expr:66*2*1.6*125*90}}}} worth of nature runes per hour if sold to Ali.

With high focus, players should expect to achieve 120-130 trips per hour using this method. This includes the time required to repair the pouch and restore stamina.

Because this method relies heavily on [[achievement diary cape]] teleports, the "Menu Entry Swapper" plugin on [[RuneLite]] is highly recommended since it allows players to configure 1-click teleports for the cape if the "Teleport submenus" setting is toggled on. Without the plugin, players must navigate a sub-menu for each teleport, though this can still be done efficiently by using keyboard shortcuts. The plugin can also be used to swap the left click option on the colossal pouch to "Empty" when outside of the bank interface and "Fill" when inside.

To begin a trip, use the achievement diary cape in your inventory to teleport to [[Kaleb Paramaya]]. Climb down the ladder, run north-east to the agility shortcut, then run north to the [[Nature Altar]]. Craft runes at the altar, teleport to the bank to retrieve more essence, then repeat the process. The player can keep the camera facing east for the entire trip.

For banking, players should use either the achievement diary cape teleport to [[Jarr]] or the [[Crafting cape]] teleport. Using the crafting cape teleport places the player a few tiles closer to the bank, but using the Jarr teleport allows the player to bring one additional essence each trip. All things considered, the Jarr teleport is very slightly (<1%) more efficient, assuming it is configured for 1-click teleports each way (e.g. left-click to Jarr and shift-click to Kaleb Paramaya).

If not using [[Runecraft cape]] or [[Max cape]], the [[Colossal pouch]] will degrade every 8 trips, which reduces its capacity from 40 to 35 essence. When this happens, players can use the [[NPC Contact]] spell to contact the [[Dark Mage]] and repair the pouch. As soon as the Dark Mage responds with "''Fine. A simple transfiguration spell should resolve things for you''", the pouch will be repaired and you may exit the dialogue without going any further.

When run energy is almost depleted, players should use the equipped [[desert amulet 4]] to teleport to [[Nardah]] and restore their stamina at the [[Elidinis Statuette]]. Ideally, this should be done immediately before the player would otherwise teleport to the bank or to Kaleb Paramaya in order to minimize the disruption to the trip.

This guide assumes players will carry 66 [[essence]] each trip (40 in the pouch + 26 in the inventory). For players under level 99, this is accomplished by equipping the achievement diary cape, attuning the [[hat of the eye]] to a [[nature tiara|nature]] or [[catalytic tiara]], and storing nature runes in the rune pouch each trip before banking. However, if navigating to the equipment tab to use the cape teleports adds even one tick on average to the trips, it is more efficient to keep the cape in the inventory and equip a weight reducing cape instead. Players with the Runecraft cape or max cape equipped can still bring 66 essence per trip, while achievement diary cape will take an inventory slot, the rune pouch does not need to be brought as the Runecraft cape removes the need for NPC contact. }} {{Money making}}

A [[crafting cape]], [[amulet of eternal glory]], [[Achievement diary cape]], or [[ring of dueling]] are great options for quick banking.

=== Digsite pendant run===
Before starting your runs, switch to the [[Standard spellbook]] and enchant all the ruby necklaces in to as many [[Digsite pendant]]s as you'll need. Alternatively, have a [[Mounted digsite pendant]] in your [[Player owned house]].

# Use a [[Digsite pendant]] to teleport to the [[Digsite]].
# Run north-west and enter the [[Earth Altar]].
# Cast [[Magic imbue]].
# Use [[Water rune]]s on the altar.
# Empty all the runecrafting pouches and use water runes on the altar again.
# Teleport to your bank of choice, storing [[Mud rune]]s and filling pouches and inventory with [[Pure essence]].

* Every '''4''' runs, replace your nearly empty [[Digsite pendant|Digsite pendant(1)]] with a new [[Digsite pendant|Digsite pendant(5)]].
* Every '''8''' runs, in addition to above, equip a new [[Binding Necklace]], and new [[ring of dueling]] if that was your bank teleport. (Current ones would have turned to dust).
* When you are low on run energy at a bank, take a [[Teleport to house (tablet)]] with you for your next run and use it to replenish energy at a [[Revitalisation pool]]. Alternatively, a player can use their ring of dueling to teleport to [[Ferox Enclave]] and begin the next run by drinking from the [[Pool of Refreshment]] to replenish run energy before teleporting to the [[digsite]].
** At 70+ agility with full graceful (minus hood), expect to use a house tablet every 8 runs (this syncs up with the binding necklace expiring)
* When your pouches degrade, withdraw 2 [[Air rune]] and a [[Cosmic rune]] and use [[NPC contact]] to contact the [[Dark Mage (Abyss)|Dark Mage]] for pouch repairs.

After completing your runs, switch back to the standard spellbook and cast [[High level alchemy]] on your remaining [[Digsite pendant|Digsite pendant(1)s]].

=== Balloon run ===
Alternatively, if access to the [[Balloon transport system]] is available it can be used to travel from Castle Wars directly to the Earth Altar for the cost of a [[Willow log]]
Before starting your runs, load up the balloon system with [[Willow log]]s. Alternatively the [[Achievement diary cape]] can be used for closer access to a bank ([[Jarr]]), and a balloon in [[Tree gnome stronghold]], along with a [[Spirit tree]] for free POH access, though it has high requirements.

This method is slightly slower than the Digsite pendant one but is considerably simpler (Note: For those without a [[Spirit tree]] in their POH, the [[Revitalisation pool]] method for restoring energy won't work as well in this run, use [[Stamina potion]]s or teleport to [[Ferox Enclave]] and use the [[Refreshment pool]])

# Starting at [[Castle Wars]], run to balloon and take it to [[Varrock]].
# Enter the nearby [[Earth Altar]].
# Cast [[Magic imbue]].
# Use [[Water rune]]s on the altar.
# Empty all the essence pouches and use water runes on the altar again.
# Teleport to [[Castle Wars]] with the [[ring of dueling]] and bank, storing [[Mud rune]]s and filling pouches and inventory with [[Pure essence]]

* Every '''8''' runs, equip a new [[Binding Necklace]] and new [[ring of dueling]] as well (Current ones would have turned to dust)

=== Notes ===
* You can replace the [[Steam battlestaff]] with a stack of [[Fire rune]]s and extra [[Water rune]]s but this will use up an essence spot, unless you have a [[Divine rune pouch]] to store the fire runes in.
* If you don't have access to a [[Revitalisation pool]], you can instead use [[Stamina potion]]s but these will typically cost more to maintain your run energy compared to [[Teleport to house (tablet)]]s
* The [[Ring of dueling]] can be replaced with any teleport near a bank
** A charged [[Amulet of glory]] to Edgeville works for a cheaper alternative but requires replacing every 4 runs and takes up an essence space
** With [[Crafting cape]] and the [[Falador diary#Hard]] completed, the crafting guild bank is a free teleport alternative that won't take up an essence space.

At average speed, one can do about 50–60 trips to the [[Nature Altar]] via the [[Abyss]] per hour, using the [[amulet of glory]] to teleport back to [[Edgeville]], and using an equipped [[pickaxe]] to mine through the rocks in the Abyss. You need to recharge your pouches every nine trips, at the [[Dark Mage (Abyss)|Dark mage]], to prevent the giant pouch from degrading.

Using the [[Small pouch|small]] through [[Giant pouch|giant]] [[essence pouch]]es, you can hold up to 54 [[essence]] per trip, resulting in a profit of {{Coins|{{GEP|Nature rune|108}} - {{GEP|Pure essence|54}}}} per trip at {{SCP|Runecraft|91}} (ignoring other expenses).

Assuming that you can make approximately 55 runs per hour, profit at lower levels using the available pouches can be expected to be roughly: {{Coins|{{GEP|Nature rune|3000}} - {{GEP|Pure essence|3000}} - {{GEP|Stamina potion(4)|4}} - {{GEP|Monkfish|10}} - {{GEP|Amulet of glory(4)|12.5}} + {{GEP|Amulet of glory|12.5}}}} at {{SCP|Runecraft|75}}, {{Coins|{{GEP|Nature rune|2150}} - {{GEP|Pure essence|2150}} - {{GEP|Stamina potion(4)|4}} - {{GEP|Monkfish|10}} - {{GEP|Amulet of glory(4)|12.5}} + {{GEP|Amulet of glory|12.5}}}} at {{SCP|Runecraft|50}} and {{Coins|{{GEP|Nature rune|1750}} - {{GEP|Pure essence|1750}} - {{GEP|Stamina potion(4)|4}} - {{GEP|Monkfish|10}} - {{GEP|Amulet of glory(4)|12.5}} + {{GEP|Amulet of glory|12.5}}}} at {{SCP|Runecraft|44}}.

The [[Mage of Zamorak]] is located low-level [[Wilderness]], near the source of the [[River Lum]]. This is a fairly safe area, but there might be [[player killer]]s, so it is advised not to take expensive items on the trips to the Abyss. The best setup would be cheap but protective gear (e.g. [[dragonhides]]). Use of [[weight-reducing clothing]] is also recommended. [[Falador shield]] can be useful to negate the loss when killed by a player, as it can recharge prayer instantly (once or twice per day) which can be used to activate [[Protect Item]]. Use of [[abyssal bracelet]] is optional to prevent [[skulling]] when entering the abyss, however {{NoCoins|{{GEP|Abyssal bracelet(5)}}}} coins is generally not worth the expense due to a 5 use limit for each. Inside the Abyss, the creatures there will be aggressive and might do fair amounts of damage. See [[Abyss#Inside the Abyss]] for more information on how to navigate the Abyss. Once inside, enter the nature rift and craft the essence in your inventory and your pouches. Teleport back to Edgeville with an amulet of glory and repeat.

Wearing a full set of [[Raiments of the Eye]] grants the player 60% additional runes when crafting nature runes, adding to the profit made from this method. No additional experience is gained, however.

A [[ring of dueling]] is optional, but it helps in case players forget to swap out an exhausted glory for a charged one. Using [[Charge dragonstone jewellery scroll]] to recharge your amulets is recommended as it is quick, allowing you to spend more time runecrafting.

The [[achievement diary cape]] is especially useful for runecrafting in the Abyss if the player has one. Teleport to [[Jarr]] at [[Shantay Pass]] for banking, then to the [[Lesser Fanatic]] to quickly reach the Abyss. Since an amulet of glory isn't needed for this method, the player could instead opt to wear a [[Desert amulet 4]] and teleport to [[Nardah]] when necessary to replenish run energy [[hitpoints]] without the need for stamina potions and food.
}}

At average speeds, one can do about 8 trips to the [[Soul Altar]] via the [[Abyss]] per hour, using the [[amulet of glory]] to teleport to [[Edgeville]], and using an equipped [[pickaxe]] to mine through the rocks in the Abyss.

Players should mine 26 [[dense essence block]]s at the [[dense runestone mine]] and then run to the [[Dark Altar]] to turn them into [[dark essence block]]s and chip them with a chisel into [[dark essence fragments]]. Then, players should run back to the dense runestone mines and mine another inventory of 25 dense essence blocks and turn into dark essence blocks before teleporting to Edgeville with an [[amulet of glory]] and going to the Soul Altar.

Once inside, enter the soul rift and craft the dark essence fragments in your inventory. Chip the remaining dark essence blocks with your chisel and craft them. Run back to the [[dense runestone mine]] and repeat. Without accounting for bonus runes from the [[Raiments of the Eye]], 204 soul runes are made per trip with this method.

|Details = 
Those with at least {{SCP|Runecraft|95}} (or 94 with [[Metztonalli white]]) can craft [[wrath rune]]s at the [[Wrath Altar]]. The Wrath Altar is only accessible to players who have completed [[Dragon Slayer II]] and cannot be accessed through the [[Abyss]].

The fastest way to craft wrath runes is via the [[mythical cape]] teleport. After using the cape to teleport to the [[Myths' guild]], players should enter the [[Myths' Guild#Basement|guild basement]], run past aggressive dragons (requiring an [[anti-dragon shield]] to minimise damage taken), and enter the cave to reach the Wrath Altar (requiring a [[wrath tiara]] or wrath attuned [[hat of the eye]]). Players can then craft their runes, teleport to a bank, and repeat the process.

Players are highly recommended to obtain the [[Raiments of the Eye]] for their [[passive effect]], which grants a bonus of 60% additional runes crafted.

Players with a [[Runecraft cape]] (req. {{SCP|Runecraft|99}}), equip the Runecraft cape but carry the mythical cape in the inventory. Using the colossal pouch, players can carry up to 66 [[essence]] per trip. Players should expect between 90 and 96 trips per hour, taking approximately ~38 seconds per trip.
The pouch won't need to be recharged because the Runecraft cape's [[Cape of Accomplishment#Perks|perk]] protects it from degrading. If the player is maxed, the [[max cape]] can be equipped for the same effect as the Runecraft cape, and also provides quick banking to the [[Crafting Guild]].

Players without a runecraft cape will follow the same route, however they will need to recharge their pouch every seven trips by using [[NPC Contact]], to prevent the pouch from degrading. 

It is worth noting that getting 96 trips per hour is a very high intensity activity requiring near perfect banking and for the wrath runes to be crafted perfectly as well. A more reasonable trip time would be ~45 seconds. Recharging your pouch and restoring health points will also contribute to a lower trip rate. It could be more relevant to expect to make between 75 and 80 trips per hour instead if not performing optimally. This results in a profit of approximately {{Coins|78*({{GEP|Wrath rune|105}}-{{GEP|Pure essence|66}})-{{GEP|Stamina potion(4)|5}}}} and {{SCP|Runecraft|41,184}}.

An alternative bank teleport option includes using the [[achievement diary cape]]. Teleport to [[Jarr]] at [[Shantay Pass]] for banking, as well as to the [[Lesser Fanatic]] to quickly reach the Dark mage in the Abyss to repair the pouch. Since an amulet of glory isn't needed for this method, the player could instead opt to wear a [[Desert amulet 4]] and teleport to [[Nardah]] when necessary to recharge run and replenish [[Prayer]] and [[Hitpoints]] without the need for stamina potions. The [[Enchanted lyre(i)]] is another option to bank, by teleporting to [[Neitiznot]], this option comes with the added benefit of not taking the cape or ring slots. This allows for the Mythical or Runecaft cape to be worn, along with either the ring of endurance or [[steel ring]], to help maintain stamina or Hitpoints. The teleport does take about 3 [[tick]]s longer to perform, but it can be closer to the bank chest than the Crafting cape teleport, and can allow taking 67 essence total.
}}

methods.wikitext:
{{Mmgtable |Activity = Creating [[Ardougne teleport (tablet)|Ardougne teleport tablets]] |Category = Skilling |SkillCategory = Magic |Image = [[File:Making magic tablets.gif]] |Input1 = Coins |Input1num = 5770 |Input1value = 1 |Input2 = Soft clay |Input2num = 1154 |Input3 = Law rune |Input3num = 2308 |Intensity = Low |Item = * [[Coins]] * [[Soft clay]] * [[Law rune]]s * [[Staff of water]] or any [[water rune]] stave |Location = [[Player-owned house]] |Members = Yes |Other = [[Player-owned house]] in [[Rimmington]]
A [[teak eagle lectern|teak eagle]], [[mahogany eagle lectern|mahogany eagle]], or [[marble lectern]] in a [[player-owned house]] [[study]] |Other Benefits = {{SCP|Magic|{{Formatexpr|1154*61}}}} |Output1 = Ardougne teleport (tablet) |Output1num = 1154 |Quest = [[Plague City]] |Skill = {{SCP|Magic|51}}
{{SCP|Construction|57}} recommended |Details = [[Ardougne teleport (tablet)|Ardougne teleport tablets]] are worth {{Coins|{{GEP|Ardougne teleport (tablet)}}}} and are generally in mid-level demand, as they are used by players with lower magic levels to reach [[Seers' Village]], among other locations. The tablets can be made on a [[teak eagle lectern|teak eagle]], [[mahogany eagle lectern|mahogany eagle]], or [[marble lectern]] in a [[player-owned house]] [[study]]. The most cost effective place to make tablets is at a house in [[Rimmington]], where there is a nearby [[NPC]] named [[Phials]] who will un-note items for 5 coins each. To make tablets, move your house to Rimmington and bring [[law rune]]s, [[note]]d [[soft clay]], and [[coins]]. Find Phials, use the noted soft clay on him, and choose the third chat option to quickly un-note them. Then enter your house through the [[POH portal|house portal]] and enchant the tablets on your lectern. For the fastest route from the entrance portal to the lectern, use [[house options]] to move your study directly north of the entrance portal, and rotate it to put the lectern on the south side of the room. This creates the shortest distance between entering your house and reaching the lectern. Use the lectern and orient your camera facing east so that the "Ardougne" icon lays on top of the lectern, which will help you select it faster. Finally, follow this process, in which the key is to keep your character moving when needed and minimise dialogues: *Use the lectern, keep your mouse over the lectern and right click the "Ardougne" icon that should appear right under it. Select "make-all" *As soon as the last soft clay is removed from your inventory, click on the POH portal until your character begins to move. *As soon as you appear in Rimmington, click towards the general store Phials is in (should be towards the right of the screen). While your character is moving, click your noted soft clay and use it on Phials. *As your character finishes running to Phials, right click the POH portal and hover over the "home" option. *When Phials' dialogue appears, press "3" on your keyboard to un-note your soft clay. *When you see the un-noted soft clay in your inventory, immediately select the "home" option you were hovering over. Wait for your character to enter the portal. *As soon as you appear in your house, click towards/on the lectern. *Repeat Each trip you will make 24 Ardougne teleport tablets for {{Coins|{{GEP|Ardougne teleport (tablet)|24}} - {{GEP|Soft clay|24}} - {{GEP|Law rune|24}} * 2 - 24 * 5}} profit, with an addition tablet on the first and last trips, as you have no tablets in the first run and will have no notes on the last run. On average, a round trip takes approximately 75-80 seconds with about 45-50 of those not requiring player input. This results in a total of 1082-1154 tablets per hour, which leads to approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Ardougne teleport (tablet)|1082}} - {{GEP|Soft clay|1082}} - {{GEP|Law rune|1082}} * 2 - 1082 * 5}} to {{Coins|{{GEP|Ardougne teleport (tablet)|1154}} - {{GEP|Soft clay|1154}} - {{GEP|Law rune|1154}} * 2 - 1154 * 5}} profit per hour. Note that Ardougne teleport tablets usually don't sell as fast as some other tablets, such as [[Varrock teleport (tablet)|Varrock teleport tablets]], but still sell consistently. An alternative to un-noting with Phials is to use a [[servant]], however this is more costly. }} {{Money making}}

{{SCP|Construction|57}} recommended |Details = [[Camelot teleport (tablet)]] tablets are worth {{Coins|{{GEP|Camelot teleport (tablet)}}}} coins and are generally in mid-level demand, as they are used by players with lower magic levels to reach [[Seers' Village]], among other locations. The tablets can be made on a [[teak eagle lectern|teak eagle]], [[mahogany eagle lectern|mahogany eagle]], or [[marble lectern]] in a [[player-owned house]] [[study]]. The most cost effective place to make tablets is at a house in [[Rimmington]], where there is a nearby [[NPC]] named [[Phials]] who will un-note items for 5 coins each. To make tablets, move your house to Rimmington and bring [[law runes]], [[note]]d [[soft clay]], and [[coins]]. Find Phials, use the noted soft clay on him, and choose the third chat option to quickly un-note them. Then enter your house through the [[POH portal|house portal]] and enchant the tablets on your lectern. For the fastest route from the entrance portal to the lectern, use [[Options#house options|house options]] to move your study directly north of the entrance portal, and rotate it to put the lectern on the south side of the room. This creates the shortest distance between entering your house and reaching the lectern. Use the lectern and orient your camera facing east so that the "Camelot" icon lays on top of the lectern, which will help you select it faster. Finally, follow this process, in which the key is to keep your character moving when needed and minimise dialogues: *Use the lectern, keep your mouse over the lectern and right click the "Camelot" icon that should appear right under it. Select "make-all" *As soon as the last soft clay is removed from your inventory, click on the POH portal until your character begins to move. *As soon as you appear in Rimmington, click towards the general store Phials is in (should be towards the right of the screen). While your character is moving, click your noted soft clay and use it on Phials. *As your character finishes running to Phials, right click the POH portal and hover over the "home" option. *When Phials' dialogue appears, press "3" on your keyboard to un-note your soft clay. *When you see the un-noted soft clay in your inventory, immediately select the "home" option you were hovering over. Wait for your character to enter the portal. *As soon as you appear in your house, click towards/on the lectern. *Repeat Each trip you will make 24 Camelot teleport tablets for {{Coins|{{GEP|Camelot teleport (tablet)|24}}-{{GEP|Soft clay|24}}-{{GEP|Law rune|24}}-24*5}} profit, with an addition tablet on the first and last trips, as you have no tablets in the first run and will have no notes on the last run. On average, a round trip takes approximately 75-80 seconds with about 45-50 of those not requiring player input. This results in a total of 1082-1154 tablets per hour, which leads to approximately {{Coins|{{GEP|Camelot teleport (tablet)|1082}}-{{GEP|Soft clay|1082}}-{{GEP|Law rune|1082}}-1082*5}} to {{Coins|{{GEP|Camelot teleport (tablet)|1154}}-{{GEP|Soft clay|1154}}-{{GEP|Law rune|1154}}-1154*5}} profit per hour. Note that Camelot teleport tablets usually don't sell as fast as some other tablets, such as [[Varrock teleport (tablet)|Varrock teleport tablets]], but still sell consistently. An alternative to un-noting with Phials is to use a [[servant]], however this is more costly. }} {{Money making}}

Up to 320 is possible with maximum efficiency. 50% more experience will also be rewarded after completing the respective Achievement Diaries. Your actual profit may be higher or lower depending on your speed and luck. Clue scrolls take additional time to complete and are not included in the profit calculation.'''

Cremating [[urium remains]], collecting the keys and opening the Gold Chests in the [[Shade Catacombs]], rewards a large number of coins and alchable items as well as a decent amount of Firemaking and Prayer experience. Chests can also reward pieces of the [[Zealot's robes]], which is the [[Prayer]] skilling outfit.

Players interested in clues can expect roughly one [[clue scroll (elite)|elite clue]] every 35 minutes. If you are very quick at completing clues this can be more profitable. For a guide that factors in the time to complete those clues see [[Money making guide/Completing elite clues (urium remains)]].

For free transportation, [[Morytania legs 3]] or better is the best way to get to Mort'ton regularly. [[Mort'ton teleport]]s are also an option, but at {{Coins|{{GEP|Mort'ton teleport}}}} each they are fairly expensive and will reduce the hourly profit by a lot, for only 20 or so seconds saved per trip. The [[File:Minigame_icon.png|frameless|width=19x19]] [[Minigame teleport]] can also be used to teleport to [[Flamtaer]] for free once every 20 minutes.

The [[gold coffin]] holds 28 remains and speeds things up considerably; it can be created with tradeable [[gold locks]] for only {{Coins|{{GEP|gold locks}}}}.

To cremate the shades:
# Fill up the gold coffin with 27 [[urium remains]] and fill the rest of the inventory with [[redwood pyre logs]] and a [[tinderbox]]. It can remain equipped in your cape slot the entire time.
# Run to the pyres, drop around 5 logs to make space, empty 5 remains into your inventory, and cremate them.
# Empty 10 more remains into your inventory with the newly available space, picking up the logs off the ground as you cremate this inventory.
# Empty the last 12 remains into your inventory and cremate them.

The fastest way to cremate remains is to alternate between two pyres. Light the first pyre, then click the second pyre 2 ticks after the XP drop. Timed correctly, there will be no delay between the logs disappearing from one pyre and appearing on the other pyre. Stack up multiple rewards on the pedestal to save time (note the loot will disappear after 5 minutes). [[Tick manipulation]] methods can be used to slightly increase the speed the shades are cremated, up to around 400 cremations + loots per hour, or 600 cremations without chests.

You can either bank the keys or go open chests directly from there. They are about the same overall speed, although saving up keys can lead to more efficient chest opening because you won't have to switch chest types as often and instead of running a partial inventory of keys while getting coin drops, more keys can be used in one run. Either way, enter the [[Shade Catacombs]], go through the gold door, and start opening chests. Each loot will give coins, swamp paste, and one other item – none of them are incredibly valuable (besides the elite clue), but they all add up to make each chest well over {{Coins|10,000}} on average.
}}

[[Pestle and mortar]]
[[Bird nest (empty)|Bird nest]]s |Quest = None |Intensity = Moderate |Other = None |Other Benefits = None |isperkill = y |kph = 5000 |kph name = Bird nests crushed per hour |Input1 = Bird nest (empty) |Input1num = 1 |Output1 = Crushed nest |Output1num = 1 |Location = [[Grand Exchange]] |Category = Processing |Members = Yes |Details = [[Crushed nest]]s are used to create [[Saradomin brew]]s. Because these are often used to train [[Herblore]] and for [[combat]], and crushing [[Bird nest (empty)|bird nest]]s gives no experience, many players avoid the tedium of making crushed nests and buy them directly from the [[Grand Exchange]]. This opens up an opportunity to crush bird nests for profit. Like all methods involving large amounts of buying and selling, which require a relatively high capital to start, it is recommended to test the market by crushing one nest first to make sure it is profitable. Multiple bird nests can be crushed in one game tick, or they can be left to auto-crush every 3 ticks. This results in anywhere between 2,000 (idle) and 5,000 (high-intensity) nests ground per hour, with profits ranging from {{Coins|2000*({{GEP|Crushed nest}}-{{GEP|Bird nest (empty)}})}} to {{Coins|5000*({{GEP|Crushed nest}}-{{GEP|Bird nest (empty)}})}}. To crush the nests, use them together with a [[pestle and mortar]]. It will slowly grind the bird nests one by one, so the speed can be significantly increased by crushing each nest manually. Doing this may be easier by placing the pestle and mortar next to the last bird nest in your inventory, reducing the distance you need to move your mouse. }} {{Money making}} [[Category:MMG/Processing]]
